title: Getting Started
code: obj <- scdown(scdown_example()) obj #> <scdown_obj> #>   cells: 48  #>   genes: 15  #>   samples: 4  #>   annotations: 6  #>   value type: counts

title: Getting Started
code: map_res <- plot_map(obj) marker_res <- explore_markers(obj, top_n = 3) gene_res <- plot_gene(obj, genes = c("CD3D", "NKG7", "LYZ")) sig_res <- plot_signature(obj, signatures = c("cytotoxic", "b_cell"))  kable(head(map_res$tables$embedding_cells, 6)) kable(marker_res$tables$top_marker_per_celltype) kable(gene_res$tables$gene_average_by_celltype) kable(sig_res$tables$signature_score_by_celltype)

title: Getting Started
code: comp_test <- test_composition(obj) sig_test <- test_signature(obj, signatures = c("cytotoxic", "b_cell"))  kable(comp_test$tables$composition_test_table) kable(sig_test$tables$signature_test_table)

title: Getting Started
code: obj_custom <- scdown(   scdown_example(),   signatures = list(my_state = c("CD3D", "IL7R")),   marker_panels = list(my_t_panel = c("CD3D", "TRAC")) )  kable(plot_signature(obj_custom, signatures = "my_state")$tables$signature_score_by_celltype)

title: Getting Started
code: report_path <- build_scdown_report(obj) report_path #> [1] "C:\\Users\\daiki\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\RtmpiCm8Mt\\scdown-report-54a058ea5669/scdown_report.html"

title: Authors and Citation
text: User D (2026). scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis Cell Annotation. R package version 0.2.0, https://github.com/dai540/scdown, https://dai540.github.io/scdown/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation},   author = {Daiki User},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0,     https://github.com/dai540/scdown},   url = {https://dai540.github.io/scdown/}, }

title: scdown
text: scdown simple downstream toolkit annotated single-cell RNA-seq data. built around one short idea: make one analysis object run fast exploratory summaries run sample-aware tests need Internally, scdown keeps data sparse-first matrix form, longer needs expand everything huge long table analysis.

title: scdown
code: install.packages("pak") pak::pak("dai540/scdown")

title: scdown
code: library(scdown)  obj <- scdown(scdown_example())  run_core(obj) build_scdown_report(obj)

title: scdown
code: plot_map(obj) explore_markers(obj) plot_gene(obj, genes = c("CD3D", "NKG7", "LYZ")) plot_composition(obj) plot_signature(obj, signatures = c("cytotoxic", "exhaustion")) infer_communication(obj)

title: scdown
code: test_markers(obj) test_composition(obj) test_signature(obj, signatures = c("cytotoxic", "b_cell")) test_communication(obj, n_perm = 25)

title: scdown
code: obj_custom <- scdown(   scdown_example(),   signatures = list(my_state = c("CD3D", "IL7R")),   marker_panels = list(my_t_panel = c("CD3D", "TRAC")),   lineage_mapping = data.frame(     pattern = c("cd4", "cd8", "nk", "b", "mono", "macro"),     lineage = c("T/NK", "T/NK", "T/NK", "B/Plasma", "Myeloid", "Myeloid")   ),   lr_pairs = data.frame(     ligand = "CCL5",     receptor = "CCR5",     pathway = "chemokine"   ) )  plot_signature(obj_custom, signatures = "my_state") check_annotation(obj_custom, panels = "my_t_panel")

title: scdown
code: obj_sce <- scdown(   sce,   annotation_col = "cell_type",   sample_col = "sample",   reduced_dim = "UMAP",   assay_name = "counts" )  obj_seurat <- scdown(   seu,   annotation_col = "cell_type",   sample_col = "sample",   reduced_dim = "umap",   assay_name = "RNA",   expression_layer = "counts" )

title: scdown
text: scdown() summarize_dataset() collapse_lineage() plot_map() explore_markers() find_markers() plot_gene() plot_composition() explore_composition() check_annotation() plot_signature() explore_signature() plot_heatmap() infer_communication() explore_communication() test_markers() test_composition() test_signature() test_communication() run_core() build_scdown_report()

what: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
title: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
code: build_scdown_report(   obj,   genes = c("CD3D", "NKG7", "LYZ", "MS4A1"),   signatures = c("cytotoxic", "exhaustion", "antigen_presentation"),   signature_sets = NULL,   run_tests = TRUE,   outdir = tempfile("scdown-report-") )

title: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
text: obj scdown_obj. genes User-selected genes. signatures User-selected signatures. signature_sets Optional custom signatures. run_tests Whether include sample-aware tests possible. outdir Output directory.

title: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report

text: obj scdown_obj. lr_pairs Optional data frame custom ligand-receptor pairs. min_detected Minimum detection rate ligand receptor. min_specificity Minimum positive enrichment rest. exclude_pattern Pattern labels exclude communication. outdir Optional output directory.

title: Explore cell-type markers — explore_markers

title: Explore cell-type markers — explore_markers
text: obj scdown_obj. top_n Number markers per cell type. exclude_pattern Pattern low-information genes exclude. min_pct_in_type Minimum detection rate inside target cell type. min_pct_diff Minimum detection-rate improvement rest. min_marker_score Minimum mean-expression improvement rest. outdir Optional output directory.

what: scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation — scdown-package
title: scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation — scdown-package
text: scdown compact downstream toolkit annotated single-cell RNA-seq data. standardizes common input types, keeps internal representation sparse-first, separates lightweight exploration sample-aware testing: maps, markers, genes interest, composition, annotation checks, signatures, communication, compact report.

title: scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation — scdown-package
Create a scdown object — scdown
code: scdown(   x,   annotation_col = "cell_type",   sample_col = "sample",   group_col = "group",   batch_col = NULL,   reduced_dim = "UMAP",   embedding = NULL,   assay_name = NULL,   expression_layer = NULL,   signatures = NULL,   marker_panels = NULL,   lineage_mapping = NULL,   lr_pairs = NULL )

title: Create a scdown object — scdown
text: x long-format expression table, list expr, optional cells, optional embedding, SingleCellExperiment / Seurat object. annotation_col Cell annotation column name. sample_col Sample column name. group_col Optional group column name. batch_col Optional batch column name. reduced_dim Name reduced dimension use. embedding Optional embedding data frame cell, UMAP1, UMAP2. assay_name Optional assay name SingleCellExperiment Seurat. expression_layer Optional Seurat layer/slot name. signatures Optional named list custom signatures. marker_panels Optional named list custom marker panels. lineage_mapping Optional data frame pattern lineage. lr_pairs Optional data frame ligand, receptor, pathway.

title: scdown 0.2.0
text: switched sparse-first internal matrix representation added custom signatures, marker panels, lineage mapping, ligand-receptor pairs split exploratory functions sample-aware testing functions added CI workflows build hygiene files
